page: Microsoft Azure L48as_v3 is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the LASv3 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage capabilities are a primary feature of this server, which includes 12,012 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong compute capabilities, particularly in multi-threaded and parallelized operations. The L48as_v3 achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Cryptographic operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by strong performance in PassMark CPU encryption tests, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Multi-threaded decompression tasks also perform in the top 25%, while memory performance is supported by a strong PassMark memory score. Single-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L48as_v3 offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring high local storage capacity and substantial memory without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. The high local storage density of over 12 TB makes it suitable for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and big data analytics. The combination of 384 GB of RAM and 48 vCPUs supports memory-intensive applications, heavy database operations, and multi-threaded processing tasks, though workloads requiring extreme single-core performance or specialized matrix math accelerators may find limitations due to the lack of AVX-512 and AMX support.
description: The Microsoft Azure L48as_v3 is a high-capacity virtual machine from the LASv3 family, designed for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. A key feature of this instance is its massive 12,012 GB of local storage, which is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core processing, encryption, and memory performance, placing it in the top 25% for multi-threaded workloads. While single-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average, the server's high resource density provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for large databases, data warehousing, and big data analytics.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L48as_v3 is a storage-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 12,012 GB of local storage, designed for large-scale database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L48as_v3 features 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 12,012 GB local storage for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and massive local storage capacity.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7763 with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores
Equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per vCPU)
Includes 12,012 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU, encryption, and multi-threaded decompression performance
Best suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and big data analytics
